feedback?

Okay, cool.
Yeah, we'll, you know, have more data to share over the next couple of weeks, but I was curious to hear what your feedback was on the last batch that we delivered.
I think, content-wise, they're strong.
There are still some issues that we're seeing on our side that might be just a part of, like, ironing out this process.
Just, like, double, like, extra periods or extra spaces or, and tell me if this is something you're seeing on our side, but, like, I'll see life cycle in two words or in one word form.

And ideally, we'll keep it as two, but I don't know if these are things that changes that maybe like are human made, basically, if it's, you know, Nora or I counteracting each other or someone, you know, one of the content editors kind of making updates, but essentially, our former rule was just one way consistently through the whole document, if it's lifecycle, two words, leave it that way, if it's one word, make it, you know, leave it that way.
But I think ideally, we're just going to do it all two words, so it's AP style.

So little things like that, where perhaps maybe those changes just haven't made their way to AI yet, or there was one other one I saw, and then Alicia, I'll kick it over to you, because I know you had more, a few more.
But there was basically, like consistency in capitalization of headlines.
If it's not, so if all other factors are equal, if it doesn't impact AEO or SEO, then the general rule for us is for headlines.
If a word is less than two letters, you can leave it lowercase.
If it's less than three letters, like if it's a, what do they call them, coordinating conjunctions, like four or and, et cetera, those can typically be lowercase as well.
And then the only reason that we would have a two or three letter word capitalized is if it's like of crucial importance for the headline.
So if you typically speaking to like the reader will capitalize that, or if it's, you know, maybe the first or last word, and then it would look funny otherwise.
But honestly, no, those are sort of the main things I've noted.
And I think a lot of that's going to come out, you know, once we've just gone through this a few cycles.
But Alish, I know there were a couple others you wanted to mention.
There we go.
It wasn't registering my click to unmute myself.
Yeah, that was another one, like the title case and the headers that I noticed.
Um, I think.
think.

So, I think if we update the writing guidelines, and we're still seeing those issues prop up, I think we can just add like a proofreader step.
Thank you.

don't

But when I kind of like checked the refutableness or kind of reliability on if it's all peer reviewed, there seemed to be some iffiness on that.
And think
That some are, most are, but I couldn't find with like 100% certainty that these always were.
And there was this one and another one, I think same, that was referenced, that was from the same like ResearchGate itself.
And so I wanted, I talked about this with Jen, but we were saying if we could kind of, I would say, not use this as the general source.
So if we can remove those ones and like kind of what if those sources that's like being linked as references and what, you know, like in this one, it's like a specific stat.
If we can remove them or find, right, like another source, I think what we always go to is anything that's .gov, .org, like a government sponsored agency or organization.
Or finding in our peer-reviewed industry journals, as long as it references an international or national agency, then we can use that.
Otherwise, we don't want to cite those sources.
So yeah, I would say for this one, and then there was another one, maybe it was this one.
There was another link.

There's another link to a research page, but same thing, like I said, if those can be removed or validated by other sources, I would say let's do that as long as it doesn't affect the integrity of the article.
And then moving forward, like I said, let's just add those as parameters, the government or organization.
And the one caveat with like industry journals would be if we...
We can have the model also say yes for journal entries, but check, but no for if it's written by any of our competitors.

Okay, okay.
I think for ResearchGate, I think we can definitely exclude that website from external linking.
And then we can also prioritize like .gov sources and like just other sources with like a specific domain.
I don't know if we can go as granular to detect if it's like written by a competitor or sponsored by a competitor.
That's something that we will have to look out for, think, manually.
Yeah.
Yeah, that's fine.
fine.
Yeah, that's fine.
Yeah, that's why we have, like, these layers still in place that we were talking about for, like, us to also, like, check.

Okay, okay, cool.
Perfect.
Thank you.
All right.
Yeah, that's all very helpful.

But, yeah, and another update that we had on the content audit side.
So that should be ready by the end of the week.
So you should be able to have a list of, like, blogs that we recommend refreshing or rewriting and blogs that we just recommend removing.
And then if you decide to continue, like, into phase two, then we can...
Like, start combining, like, refreshes or just rewriting existing content with, like, the content that we're doing that's, like, brand new.
But, yeah, that is, so we, ideally, we would have, like, a response by next Monday, just to understand if, like, we're going to be continuing or not.
But, yeah, I think we have, you know, a good foundation of content and, like, the early signals are really positive.
So, yeah, I think we would be in a really good spot if we decide to continue.

So, the, as far as the continued relationship, it's a monthly payment, but is it something where we agree to it for, like, the entirety of the year, or is it month by month?
Um, Eric, correct me if I'm wrong, but I think it's a one-time payment.
I think it's a 12-month contract that is signed at this stage, but I can double-check with our head of customer ops and get back to you.
Okay.
I think that's what was reflected in the last document I looked at.
That matches what I remember.
But yeah, if you don't mind double-checking, I'd appreciate just so I can kind of know how to present it to him.
I think from my understanding, we're intending to move forward.
But I know we've been looking at a lot of MarTech solutions lately, and I want to make sure he understands where there's overlap and has an informed decision to make.
But no, think preliminarily, I can tell you, I think the plan is to move forward.
I'll have to confirm details, but I don't know otherwise.
Okay.
Okay.
Perfect.
Yeah, think I can double-check to see, like, what the exact terms are.
Okay.
Okay.
Cool.
Cool.
Yeah.
So like I said, we'll be in touch tomorrow with the new blogs.
So, you know, feel free to tag us, leave comments.
And yeah, we'll update the writing guidelines and go from there.
Awesome.
I can also, I mean, if it's helpful, I'm working on finalizing the tone of voice document we have at Rimkus.
I think we gave you all like a draft version of that.
If it would be helpful just to forward the updated version along when we get it, I can send it your way.
Yes.
Yes, please.
Yeah.
That's always helpful to have.
Okay.
And for the article specifically that I mentioned, I can put it in the chat here.
Can we then just like have that go back to editing to like check those, the research gate and like remove them?
And then I'll just leave it in draft form as is and like update it later.
Cool.
And then, yeah, I see the other ones that are in the drafts, too.
fun that we were, like, rolling on that.
I think it was just the initial, maybe, like, confusion, Jen, with, like, the tally of, like, the schedule or programming.
But now that we're kind of, like, caught up, I think that works.
And then, yeah, myself, we can work at our own pace with that.
Yeah, yeah.
Honestly, it was my fault because I was confused with 1Password.
So, whenever we, so we store all of our, like, CMS credentials in 1Password.
So, we don't share, like, passwords on, like, Slack emails and whatever.
And I saved it, like, under employee because I thought, I was like, oh, it's for employees.
But it turns out it was just me, the employee.
So, the person who was supposed to be doing the staging, he did not have access to the credentials.
So, I just had to change the access permission.
So, that was the reason for the delay.
But, yeah, it's all sorted.
So, it shouldn't be an issue.
I was just, I was just.
So, you know, was on with Jen earlier and started the call.
It's like, let's talk about things.
And then I'm like, oh, wait, I just reset my password to WordPress.
Like, I have to think about it for a second.

like, your activity is suspicious.

It's not that suspicious.
Yeah, yeah.

So there's just, like, you never really know, like, with these CMS platforms, what you're going to get.
Yeah, yep.
So just be ready with all the passwords and numbers and everything.
Thank Thank

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance, provide feedback, and align on process improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance:** Early results are strong, with 5 new blogs ranking for \~120 keywords. The "New York City inspection" article leads in clicks.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)
  - [**Process Feedback:** Rimkus requested stricter adherence to style guides (e.g., "life cycle" as two words, specific headline capitalization) and a ban on unreliable sources like ResearchGate.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [**Action Plan:** GrowthX will update writing guidelines, resubmit the "Net Zero Architecture" article for source correction, and implement a redline review process to track Rimkus's edits.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=602.0)
  - [**Next Steps:** A content audit report is due this week. Rimkus must confirm continuation by Monday, Nov 3, pending clarification on the contract term (12-month vs. month-to-month).](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance & Backlog

  - [**Performance:** The first 5 blogs are performing well, ranking for \~120 keywords and driving organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)
      - [**Top Performer:** "New York City inspection" article (most clicks).](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=320.0)
      - [**Top Position:** "Best practices for sustainable MEP design" (highest average Google position).](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=306.0)
  - [**Backlog:** 5 new blogs are in editing, due tomorrow. This creates a publishing buffer to maintain a consistent schedule.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [**GA4 Issue:** Blog URLs are not appearing in the GA4 performance table. GrowthX is investigating.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=230.0)

### Process Feedback & Style Guide Updates

  - [Rimkus requested stricter adherence to style guides to improve quality and consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [**Specific Style Rules:**](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
      - [**"Life cycle":** Must be two words (AP style).](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
      - [**Headline Capitalization:**](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
          - Words \<3 letters (e.g., "for," "and") → lowercase.
          - Words \<2 letters (e.g., "a," "an") → lowercase.
          - [Exceptions: First/last word, or words of crucial importance.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
  - [**Source Verification:**](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)
      - [**Problem:** The "Net Zero Architecture Strategies" article cited ResearchGate, which Rimkus deemed unreliable due to inconsistent peer-review.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=682.0)
      - [**New Rule:** Exclude ResearchGate. Prioritize .gov, .org, and peer-reviewed industry journals.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=801.0)
      - [**Competitor Content:** GrowthX will manually check for competitor bylines (e.g., JS Held), as AI detection is not feasible.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=895.0)

### Content Audit & Contract Renewal

  - [**Audit Report:** Due end-of-week, identifying content for refresh, rewrite, or removal.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)
  - [**Renewal Decision:** Rimkus must decide on continuation by Monday, Nov 3.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)
      - [**Contract Term:** Jennifer Bulmash needs clarification on the term (12-month vs. month-to-month) to present the decision to Vitaly.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1055.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Rimkus plans to move forward but needs to assess overlap with other MarTech solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1108.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida Knežević (GrowthX):**](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=602.0)
      - [Update writing guidelines with new style and source rules.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=979.0)
      - [Resubmit "Net Zero Architecture" article for source correction.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1182.0)
      - [Implement redline review process to track Rimkus's edits.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=559.0)
      - [Clarify contract terms with Customer Ops.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1055.0)
      - [Deliver 5 new blog drafts by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
      - [Deliver content audit report by end-of-week.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)
  - [**Jennifer Bulmash (Rimkus):**](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1159.0)
      - [Send finalized Rimkus tone-of-voice document to GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=1159.0)
      - [Confirm renewal decision by Monday, Nov 3.](https://fathom.video/share/LpnyXgxazxbPD6YjV1gDrCJByY6uvqgC?tab=summary&timestamp=994.0)

action_items.description: Email editor re: lifecycle 2 words; headline title case; spacing/link formatting
Update writing guidelines: exclude ResearchGate; prioritize .gov/.org/peer-reviewed; add competitor-byline check; add proofread step
Confirm contract/payment terms w/ Head of Customer Ops; update Jennifer
Share updated tone-of-voice doc w/ Aida
Share 'Net Zero Architecture Strategies' link in chat; keep in draft; send to editing to remove ResearchGate/replace sources
